concerns.text: The plan cites HuggingFace Parquet files (e.g., 'defects4j-context-5-len-10000-prompt-3') as the primary source for 'project metadata and bug commit history' to identify buggy files. These datasets are typically formatted for LLM prompt engineering (context/prompt pairs) and do not contain the granular file-level commit history or the raw source code required to compute static metrics (Cyclomatic Complexity, Halstead Volume). The plan assumes raw Java source code will be retrieved via 'git clone' from 'associated source links,' but the Parquet files themselves do not provide the necessary ground-truth mapping between specific file paths and bug-fix commits. Using these prompt-engineering datasets as the primary source for bug labeling is inappropriate; the canonical Defects4J framework (via `git clone` of the official repo and `defects4j` CLI) is required to reliably extract the file-level bug labels needed for FR-003.
